sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Still wrong. Current state:
- Red box: [p, b, c, Y] = [purple, blue, charcoal, yellow]
- Green box: [O, R, N, M] = [orange, red, green, pink]
- Not completed
I've tried 9 hypotheses now. Let me think about what I haven't tried.
Let me list all attempts again:
1. Top panel order, red first: Red=[O,p,c,R], Green=[b,N,Y,M] - WRONG
2. Bottom palette order, red first: Red=[R,p,c,N], Green=[O,M,b,Y] - WRONG
3. Between anchors: Red=[R,b,c,N], Green=[O,p,Y,M] - WRONG
4. Top panel order, green first: Green=[O,p,R,b], Red=[N,c,Y,M] - WRONG
5. Bottom palette, column-by-column: Red=[R,N,c,M], Green=[p,O,b,Y] - WRONG
6. Bottom palette order, green first: Green=[R,p,N,O], Red=[M,c,b,Y] - WRONG
7. Warm/cool: Red=[O,c,R,Y], Green=[p,b,N,M] - GAME OVER
8. Bottom palette even/odd: Red=[p,c,O,b], Green=[R,N,M,Y] - WRONG
9. Top panel even/odd (even→green, odd→red): Red=[p,c,b,Y], Green=[O,R,N,M] - WRONG
Let me think about what I haven't tried:
- Top panel even/odd reversed: even→red, odd→green
  Red: O, R, N, M (4 colors but only 3 slots) - doesn't work
- What about the BOTTOM palette even/odd reversed?
  Even→red: R, N, M, Y (4 colors but only 3 slots) - doesn't work
What if the ordering within each box is not left-to-right but some other pattern?
What if it's right-to-left?
Top panel: O, p, R, b, N, Y, M
Red box (first 3, right-to-left): R, p, O → [R, p, c, O]
Green box (last 4, right-to-left): M, Y, N, b → [M, Y, N, b]
Or what if the boxes are filled in a zigzag pattern?
Or what if the fill order is based on the bottom palette but in reverse?
Bottom palette reversed: Y, b, M, O, N, p, R
Red (first 3): Y, b, M → [Y, c, b, M]
Green (last 4): O, N, p, R → [O, N, p, R]
Let me try yet another approach. What if the rule involves the POSITION of the charcoal dot?
The charcoal dot is at position 2 (0-indexed) in the red box.
What if the red box should mirror positions from the top panel?
Top panel position 2 is R. What if the red box should have R at position 2? But position 2 is charcoal...
What if the charcoal represents the "center" and the box should be symmetric around it?
Red box has 4 slots: positions 0, 1, 2(charcoal), 3
If symmetric around position 2, then pos 1 and pos 3 should be the same color? That seems unlikely.
What if I should think about this in terms of the BOTTOM palette arrangement?
Bottom palette: R, p, N, O, M, b, Y
The bottom palette has 7 items. What if:
- The middle item (position 3: O) goes to the charcoal position (but it's already charcoal, so maybe O is excluded)
- Items to the left of O: R, p, N → go to red box (3 items)
- Items to the right of O: M, b, Y → go to green box (3 items, but green has 4 slots)
Doesn't work.
What if:
- Items to the left of O (inclusive): R, p, N, O → 4 items → green box
- Items to the right of O: M, b, Y → 3 items → red box
Green: [R, p, N, O]
Red: [M, c, b, Y]
I tried this as hypothesis 6!
What if:
- Items to the left of O: R, p, N → 3 items → red box
- Items to the right of O (inclusive): O, M, b, Y → 4 items → green box
Red: [R, p, c, N]
Green: [O, M, b, Y]
I tried this as hypothesis 2!
Let me try a completely different approach. What if the answer is related to COLOR WHEEL ordering or some property of the colors themselves?
- O (orange): warm, secondary
- p (purple): cool, secondary
- R (red): warm, primary
- b (blue): cool, primary
- N (green): cool, primary
- Y (yellow): warm, primary
- M (pink/magenta): warm, secondary
Primary colors: R, b, N, Y (4 colors)
Secondary colors: O, p, M (3 colors)
Red box (3 slots): secondary colors → [O, c, p, M]
Green box (4 slots): primary colors → [R, b, N, Y]
Let me try this!
